update_activity.name: Update Activity (deprecated)
update_activity.description: Update a PushWard Live Activity state and content.

state_text.name: State Text
state_text.description: Text shown as the activity state.

icon.name: Icon
icon.description: SF Symbol or MDI icon name (e.g. arrow.down.circle.fill, mdi:download).

subtitle.name: Subtitle
subtitle.description: Subtitle text shown on the activity.

accent_color.name: Accent Color
accent_color.description: Accent color for the activity. Hex (#RRGGBB) or named color.

url.name: URL
url.description: Primary URL shown as a tappable button. http(s) or a custom scheme such as homeassistant://.

secondary_url.name: Secondary URL
secondary_url.description: Secondary URL shown as a second tappable button.

end_date.name: End Date
end_date.description: Unix timestamp. Countdown: when the countdown ends. Live progress (generic and steps): when the bar finishes animating.

severity_label.name: Severity Label
severity_label.description: Override the Info/Warning/Critical badge text. Alert template only.

value.name: Value
value.description: Gauge: numeric value. Timeline: labeled values object (e.g. Temp=22.5).

completion_message.name: Completion Message
completion_message.description: Message shown when the activity ends.

scale.name: Scale
scale.description: Y-axis scale mode (linear or logarithmic). Timeline template only.

decimals.name: Decimal Places
decimals.description: Decimal places for value display (0-10). Timeline template only.

smoothing.name: Smooth Lines
smoothing.description: Enable curve interpolation between data points. Timeline template only.

thresholds.name: Thresholds
thresholds.description: Horizontal reference lines on the sparkline. Timeline template only.

sound.name: Sound
sound.description: Notification sound played on the device for this update.

priority.name: Priority Override
priority.description: Per-update priority (0-10). Overrides the activity's configured priority for this push only.

background_color.name: Background Color
background_color.description: Background color for the Live Activity. Hex (#RRGGBB) or named color.

text_color.name: Text Color
text_color.description: Text color for the Live Activity. Hex (#RRGGBB) or named color.

warning_threshold.name: Warning Threshold
warning_threshold.description: Seconds before end_date when the countdown enters warning state. Countdown template only.

alarm.name: Alarm
alarm.description: Trigger AlarmKit alarm on countdown expiry (iOS 26+, countdown template only).

snooze_seconds.name: Snooze Seconds
snooze_seconds.description: How long the iOS Snooze button extends the countdown (60–3600). Defaults to 300 (5 min). Requires alarm (iOS 26+, countdown template only).

step_labels.name: Step Labels
step_labels.description: Ordered list of step labels. Length must equal total_steps. Steps template only.

step_rows.name: Step Rows
step_rows.description: Ordered list of row heights (1-10) per step. Length must equal total_steps. Steps template only.

step_weights.name: Step Weights
step_weights.description: Ordered list of relative step widths, all positive. Length must equal total_steps. Steps template only.

step_colors.name: Step Colors
step_colors.description: Ordered list of per-step colors, named or hex. An empty entry uses the accent color. Length must equal total_steps. Steps template only.

live_progress.name: Live Progress
live_progress.description: Animate the progress bar toward End Date between updates. Generic and steps templates; steps also needs Start Date.

fired_at.name: Fired At
fired_at.description: Unix timestamp when the alert was triggered. Alert template only.

units.name: Units Map
units.description: Per-series unit labels. Keys must match value dict keys. Timeline template only.

duration.name: Duration
duration.description: Set-and-forget alternative to End Date: seconds (>=1) or a duration string like "1h30m". The server re-anchors start/end to now. Send either Duration or End Date — Duration wins if both are set.

start_date.name: Start Date
start_date.description: Unix timestamp. Countdown: when the countdown began; the server defaults this to now. Steps live progress: when the current step began.

history.name: History (seed)
history.description: Initial sparkline seed: an object of series name to a list of (timestamp, value). Seed-only — the server takes over after the first update.

tap_action.name: Tap Action
tap_action.description: Whole-activity tap target as an object: url (required), optional foreground, and for an http(s) url a silent webhook via method/headers/body.

url_action.name: Primary Button
url_action.description: Primary tappable button as an object: url (required), optional title, icon, foreground, and http(s) method/headers/body for a silent webhook.

secondary_url_action.name: Secondary Button
secondary_url_action.description: Secondary tappable button — same object shape as the Primary Button.

ended_ttl.name: Ended TTL
ended_ttl.description: Seconds to keep the activity after it ends.

stale_ttl.name: Stale TTL
stale_ttl.description: Seconds before the activity is considered stale.

dismissal_ttl.name: Dismissal TTL
dismissal_ttl.description: Seconds the ended activity lingers on the Lock Screen before it is removed. 0 removes it immediately on end.

countdown_options.name: Countdown options
countdown_options.description: Only used by the countdown template.

steps_options.name: Steps options
steps_options.description: Only used by the steps template.

alert_options.name: Alert options
alert_options.description: Only used by the alert template.

gauge_timeline_options.name: Gauge & Timeline options
gauge_timeline_options.description: Only used by the gauge and timeline templates.

action_buttons.name: Action buttons
action_buttons.description: Tappable URLs and rich button / tap actions for the activity.

display_options.name: Display, color & sound
display_options.description: Optional styling and per-update overrides. Apply to any template.

update_activity_board.name: Update Activity (Board)
update_activity_board.description: Update a PushWard board Live Activity (1–4 tiles).

tiles.name: Tiles
tiles.description: List of 1–4 tiles. Each: label (required, ≤32), value (required string, ≤16), and optional unit (≤8), icon (SF Symbol or mdi:), color (named or hex), trend (up/down/flat), url_action object.

state_text.name: State Text
state_text.description: Text shown as the activity state.

subtitle.name: Subtitle
subtitle.description: Subtitle text shown on the activity.

icon.name: Icon
icon.description: SF Symbol or MDI icon name (e.g. square.grid.2x2.fill, mdi:view-dashboard).

completion_message.name: Completion Message
completion_message.description: Message shown when the activity ends.

accent_color.name: Accent Color
accent_color.description: Accent color for the activity. Hex (#RRGGBB) or named color.

background_color.name: Background Color
background_color.description: Background color for the Live Activity. Hex (#RRGGBB) or named color.

text_color.name: Text Color
text_color.description: Text color for the Live Activity. Hex (#RRGGBB) or named color.

tap_action.name: Tap Action
tap_action.description: Whole-activity tap target as an object: url (required), optional foreground, and for an http(s) url a silent webhook via method/headers/body. (Per-tile taps go in each tile's url_action.)

sound.name: Sound
sound.description: Notification sound played on the device for this update.

priority.name: Priority Override
priority.description: Per-update priority (0-10). Overrides the activity's configured priority for this push only.

ended_ttl.name: Ended TTL
ended_ttl.description: Seconds to keep the activity after it ends.

stale_ttl.name: Stale TTL
stale_ttl.description: Seconds before the activity is considered stale.

dismissal_ttl.name: Dismissal TTL
dismissal_ttl.description: Seconds the ended activity lingers on the Lock Screen before it is removed. 0 removes it immediately on end.

update_activity_log.name: Update Activity (Log)
update_activity_log.description: Update a PushWard log Live Activity (1–20 newest-first lines).

lines.name: Lines
lines.description: List of 1–20 newest-first log lines. Each: text (required, ≤512), and optional at (unix timestamp) and level (info/warn/error).

state_text.name: State Text
state_text.description: Text shown as the activity state.

subtitle.name: Subtitle
subtitle.description: Subtitle text shown on the activity.

icon.name: Icon
icon.description: SF Symbol or MDI icon name (e.g. list.bullet.rectangle, mdi:format-list-bulleted).

completion_message.name: Completion Message

text_color.description: Text color for the Live Activity. Hex (#RRGGBB) or named color.

tap_action.name: Tap Action
tap_action.description: Whole-activity tap target as an object: url (required), optional foreground, and for an http(s) url a silent webhook via method/headers/body.

sound.name: Sound
sound.description: Notification sound played on the device for this update.

priority.name: Priority Override
priority.description: Per-update priority (0-10). Overrides the activity's configured priority for this push only.

ended_ttl.name: Ended TTL
ended_ttl.description: Seconds to keep the activity after it ends.

stale_ttl.name: Stale TTL
stale_ttl.description: Seconds before the activity is considered stale.

dismissal_ttl.name: Dismissal TTL
dismissal_ttl.description: Seconds the ended activity lingers on the Lock Screen before it is removed. 0 removes it immediately on end.

create_activity.name: Create Activity
create_activity.description: Create a new PushWard Live Activity.

ended_ttl.name: Ended TTL
ended_ttl.description: Seconds to keep the activity after it ends.

stale_ttl.name: Stale TTL
stale_ttl.description: Seconds before the activity is considered stale.

dismissal_ttl.name: Dismissal TTL
dismissal_ttl.description: Seconds the ended activity lingers on the Lock Screen before it is removed. 0 removes it immediately on end.

completion_message.name: Completion Message
completion_message.description: Optional message shown when the activity ends.

widget_refresh.name: Refresh Widget
widget_refresh.description: Force-refresh a tracked PushWard widget. Bypasses the in-memory diff cache so the widget is re-rendered and pushed to iOS even when the value hasn't changed.

slug.name: Slug
slug.description: Widget slug identifier. Either slug or entity_id must be provided.

entity_id.name: Entity
entity_id.description: HA entity bound to the widget. Either slug or entity_id must be provided.

send_notification.name: Send Notification
send_notification.description: Send a push notification via PushWard.

subtitle.name: Subtitle
subtitle.description: Optional subtitle shown below the title.

level.name: Level
level.description: iOS interruption level. Controls how the notification is delivered.

volume.name: Volume
volume.description: Sound volume for critical alerts (0.0-1.0). Only used when level is critical.

thread_id.name: Thread ID
thread_id.description: Groups notifications together in Notification Center.

collapse_id.name: Collapse ID
collapse_id.description: APNs deduplication key. Replaces previous notification with the same key.

source.name: Source
source.description: Source identifier for grouping in the PushWard inbox.

source_display_name.name: Source Display Name
source_display_name.description: Human-readable source name shown in the PushWard inbox.

activity_slug.name: Activity Slug
activity_slug.description: Link this notification to an existing Live Activity.

push.name: Push
push.description: Send as an APNs push alert to all devices. When false, the notification is stored in the inbox only.

url.name: URL
url.description: URL to open when the notification is tapped.

media.name: Media
media.description: Media attachment as an object with `url` (HTTPS) and `type` (image, video, or audio).

icon_url.name: Icon URL
icon_url.description: HTTPS URL of a custom per-notification icon.

metadata.name: Metadata
metadata.description: Key-value pairs of additional metadata attached to the notification.

actions.name: Actions
actions.description: List of action buttons. Each action is an object with id, title, optional url, foreground, destructive, authentication_required, and icon. For an http(s) url you can also send a silent webhook with method (GET/POST/PUT/PATCH/DELETE/HEAD), headers, and body. Set text_input: true on a silent http(s) action to prompt for a typed reply (optional text_input_placeholder and text_input_button_title); the reply is sent to your webhook as JSON with a text field. To embed it in body instead, use the {{input}} placeholder and wrap the body in a Jinja raw block in your automation, so Home Assistant does not render it away before the action is sent.

send_email.name: Send Email
send_email.description: Send a transactional email via PushWard. Provide a text body, an HTML body, or both. The integration key needs the "emails" capability.

to.name: To
to.description: Recipient email address. Must be an address you have already added and confirmed in the PushWard iOS app — unverified addresses are rejected.

body.name: Body
body.description: Plain-text email body. Provide this, an HTML body, or both.

html_body.name: HTML Body
html_body.description: HTML email body. Provide this, a plain-text body, or both.

delete_widget.name: Delete Widget
delete_widget.description: Delete a PushWard widget from the server. If a tracked-widget config entry still drives this slug, the widget is recreated on the next restart/sync — remove the config entry to delete it permanently. Provide either slug or entity_id.

slug.name: Slug
slug.description: Widget slug identifier. Either slug or entity_id must be provided.

entity_id.name: Entity
entity_id.description: HA entity bound to the widget. Resolved to the matching widget by config. Either slug or entity_id must be provided.

widget_not_found.message: No PushWard widget matches slug={slug} entity_id={entity_id}.

user.title: Connect to PushWard
user.description: Enter your PushWard integration key to get started. Get the PushWard iOS app from the App Store: {app_store_url}

data_description.integration_key: Open the PushWard iOS app (App Store: {app_store_url}) → Settings → Integration Keys. You can paste the default key, or create a new one with scope 'ha-*'.
Create one in the PushWard iOS app under Settings → Integration Keys with scope 'ha-*'.

reauth_confirm.title: Reauthenticate
reauth_confirm.description: The PushWard integration key is invalid. Please provide a new one.

data_description.integration_key: Create one in the PushWard iOS app under Settings → Integration Keys with scope 'ha-*'.

error.cannot_connect: Failed to connect to PushWard server.
error.invalid_url: URL must use http:// or https:// scheme.

data_description.template: Activity layout template. 'generic' works for most entities.

data_description.start_states: Entity states that start the Live Activity. Select from the list or type custom values.
data_description.end_states: Entity states that end the Live Activity. Select from the list or type custom values.
data_description.remaining_time_entity: Read remaining time from a separate entity (e.g. a washer's time sensor). Leave empty to use the tracked entity. Auto-parses a timestamp (finish-time) sensor, a duration sensor with units, an H:MM:SS string, or plain seconds.
data_description.remaining_time_attribute: Attribute holding remaining time (on the source entity, or the tracked entity if no source is set). Leave empty to use the source entity's state. Example: remaining
data_description.current_step_entity: Read the current step from a separate entity. Leave empty to use the tracked entity.
data_description.current_step_attribute: Attribute holding the current step as an integer (on the source entity, or the tracked entity if no source is set).
data_description.severity_label: Override the Info/Warning/Critical badge text shown on the alert. Leave empty to use the severity name. Max 32 characters. Alert template only.
data_description.min_value: Minimum value for the gauge range.
data_description.max_value: Maximum value for the gauge range.
data_description.unit: Display unit shown next to the value (e.g. °C, %, kW). Max 32 characters.
data_description.series_entities: Bind separate entities as named lines on one chart (e.g. a PM2.5 sensor per room). Format: [Label=]entity_id[:attribute], comma-separated. The label defaults to the entity's friendly name; read an attribute with entity_id:attribute. Combines with Series; up to 10 lines total.
data_description.completion_message: Text shown when the activity ends. Leave empty for 'Complete'. Example: Wash Done
data_description.warning_threshold: Seconds before the end of a countdown when the server sends a warning push. Leave empty to disable. Countdown template only.
data_description.alarm: Trigger an AlarmKit alarm when the countdown expires (iOS 26+). Countdown template only.
data_description.snooze_seconds: How long the iOS Snooze button extends the countdown (60–3600). Leave empty for the 300s (5 min) default. Requires Alarm. Countdown template only.
data_description.tiles: Board tiles (1–4), each reading a separate entity. Format: Label=entity_id:attribute:unit:icon (attribute, unit, icon optional). Example: Temp=sensor.kitchen::°C, Door=binary_sensor.front_door. Board template only.
data_description.log_columns: Extra values appended to each log line (max 6), comma-separated. Format: [Label=]source[|unit]. source = an attribute of this entity (brightness), another entity's state (binary_sensor.door), or another entity's attribute (sensor.temp:temperature). Example: color_temp_kelvin|K, brightness → 'On · 4000K · 153'. Log template only.

data_sources.name: Data sources
data_sources.description: Bind other entities or attributes that feed values into this activity.

data_description.progress_entity: Read progress from a separate entity instead of the tracked entity. Leave empty to use the tracked entity. With the attribute field set, reads that attribute of this entity; otherwise its state.
data_description.progress_attribute: Attribute holding a 0–100 progress value (on the source entity, or the tracked entity if no source is set). Example: percentage
data_description.remaining_time_entity: Read remaining time from a separate entity (e.g. a washer's time sensor). Leave empty to use the tracked entity. Auto-parses a timestamp (finish-time) sensor, a duration sensor with units, an H:MM:SS string, or plain seconds.
data_description.remaining_time_attribute: Attribute holding remaining time (on the source entity, or the tracked entity if no source is set). Leave empty to use the source entity's state. Example: remaining
data_description.live_progress: Fill the progress bar smoothly and count down an ETA to the finish time on the device, using the remaining-time source above. Needs a remaining-time entity or attribute. On the steps template it fills the current step rather than the whole run.
data_description.value_entity: Read the gauge value from a separate entity. Leave empty to use the tracked entity. Uses the source entity's state unless an attribute is set.
data_description.value_attribute: Attribute holding the current gauge value as a number (on the source entity, or the tracked entity if no source is set). Leave empty to use the entity's state.
data_description.fired_at_entity: Read the alert's fired-at time from a separate entity. Leave empty to use the tracked entity. Accepts a unix timestamp or an ISO timestamp sensor. Alert template only.
data_description.fired_at_attribute: Attribute holding the unix timestamp when the alert fired (on the source entity, or the tracked entity if no source is set). Alert template only.
data_description.subtitle_entity: Show the subtitle from a separate entity (e.g. a program/mode sensor). Leave empty to use the tracked entity. Uses the source entity's state unless an attribute is set.
data_description.subtitle_attribute: Attribute to show as subtitle (on the source entity, or the tracked entity if no source is set). Leave empty to use the source entity's state, or the friendly name when no source is set.
data_description.series: Map entity attributes to named series. Format: attribute=Label, attribute2=Label2. Leave empty to use the entity's state as a single series.
data_description.log_level_attribute: Attribute supplying each log line's level (info/warn/error). Leave empty for no level tag. Log template only.

data_description.step_labels: Labels for each step. Format: 1=First Step, 2=Second Step. Must cover steps 1..total_steps. Steps template only.
data_description.step_rows: Row height (1–10) for each step, comma-separated. Must have total_steps entries. Steps template only.
data_description.step_weights: Relative width of each step, comma-separated. Example: 1, 2, 1 makes the middle step twice as wide. All values must be positive. Must have total_steps entries. Steps template only.
data_description.step_colors: Color of each step, comma-separated (named or hex). Example: green, , red leaves the middle step on the accent color. Must have total_steps entries. Steps template only.

data_description.units: Per-series unit labels. Format: SeriesLabel=unit, Temperature=°C, Humidity=%. Timeline template only.
data_description.primary_series: Label of the series shown as the headline value and used for the compact high/low range. Leave empty to use the tracked entity's own series (or the first configured series).
data_description.scale: Y-axis scale mode for the sparkline chart.
data_description.decimals: Number of decimal places shown for values (0–10).
data_description.smoothing: Enable curve interpolation between data points for a smoother sparkline.
data_description.thresholds: Horizontal reference lines on the sparkline. Format: value:color:label. Example: 25:red:Hot, 18:blue:Cold. Max 5.
data_description.history_period: Minutes of past data to seed the sparkline when the activity starts. Numeric-state entities (plain sensors, value entities, and state-sourced series entities) backfill from Home Assistant's recorder; attribute-based series fill only from samples collected while the integration runs, because Home Assistant stopped recording most attributes in 2024.8. Set to 0 to start with no history.

data_description.slug: Unique identifier for this activity. Leave empty to auto-generate from entity ID. Example: ha-living-room-lamp
data_description.activity_name: Display name shown on the iPhone Live Activity. Defaults to the entity's friendly name.
data_description.icon: Override icon. Leave empty to use the entity’s native icon (e.g. mdi:thermometer). SF Symbols also supported (e.g. lamp.desk.fill).
data_description.icon_attribute: Entity attribute for dynamic icon. Overrides static icon and entity icon when set.
data_description.priority: Higher priority activities are kept longer when the device limit is reached (0 = lowest, 10 = highest).
data_description.sound: Sound played on every update for this entity. Leave empty to use no override.
data_description.update_interval: Minimum seconds between updates. Rapid state changes are throttled.
data_description.state_labels: Custom display text for states. Format: state=Label, state2=Label 2. Example: heating=Heating Up, idle=Standby

data_description.accent_color: Accent color for the Live Activity. Leave empty for default blue.
data_description.accent_color_attribute: Entity attribute for dynamic accent color. Overrides static color. Supports RGB, HS, XY, Kelvin, named colors, and hex.
data_description.background_color: Background color for the Live Activity. Leave empty for default.
data_description.background_color_attribute: Entity attribute for dynamic background color. Overrides static color. Supports RGB, HS, XY, Kelvin, named colors, and hex.
data_description.text_color: Text color for the Live Activity. Leave empty for default.
data_description.text_color_attribute: Entity attribute for dynamic text color. Overrides static color.

data_description.tap_action_url: Optional URL opened when tapping the Live Activity background. Supports http(s) (opens Safari) or custom schemes like homeassistant://navigate/lovelace/0 (opens that app).
data_description.tap_action_foreground: On: tap opens the URL. Off: silent webhook (http(s) only) — fires a POST in the background without leaving the Lock Screen.
data_description.url: Primary button URL. Steps and alert templates only. Supports http(s) or custom schemes.
data_description.url_foreground: On: tap opens the URL. Off: silent webhook (http(s) only).
data_description.secondary_url: Secondary button URL. Steps and alert templates only.
data_description.secondary_url_foreground: On: tap opens the URL. Off: silent webhook (http(s) only).

data_description.ended_ttl: Seconds after the activity ends before the server auto-deletes it. This also controls how soon the ended card leaves the iPhone Lock Screen (iOS caps it at 4 hours regardless). Leave empty to keep indefinitely. Example: 300
data_description.stale_ttl: Seconds of inactivity before the server auto-ends the activity. Leave empty to disable. Example: 1800
data_description.dismissal_ttl: Seconds the ended activity lingers on the Lock Screen before it is removed. 0 removes it immediately on end. Example: 60

data_description.template: Activity layout template. 'generic' works for most entities.

error.invalid_url: URL must include a valid scheme. Use http(s):// or a custom app scheme like homeassistant://.
error.silent_requires_http: Silent webhook (foreground off) requires an http:// or https:// URL.
error.invalid_gauge_range: Minimum value must be less than maximum value.
error.tiles_required: At least one tile is required for the board template.
error.too_many_series: A timeline supports at most 10 series in total (series attributes plus series entities).
error.step_length_mismatch: Step rows, weights, and colors must each have exactly one entry per step (matching total steps).
error.invalid_step_rows: Step rows must be whole numbers, one per step (e.g. 1, 2, 3).
error.invalid_step_weights: Step weights must be positive numbers, one per step (e.g. 1, 2.5, 1).
error.invalid_step_colors: Step colors must be named colors or hex like #ff0000; leave a slot blank to keep the accent color.
error.invalid_tile: Each tile must be label=entity_id (e.g. CPU=sensor.cpu_temp).
error.too_many_tiles: A board supports at most 4 tiles.
error.invalid_series_entity: Each series entity must reference an entity_id (e.g. Bedroom=sensor.bedroom_pm25).
error.invalid_log_column: Each column needs a source: an attribute, entity_id, or entity_id:attribute.
error.too_many_log_columns: A log line supports at most 6 columns.
error.invalid_threshold: Each threshold must start with a number (e.g. 25:red:Hot).
error.too_many_thresholds: A timeline supports at most 5 thresholds.
error.unknown_primary_series: Primary series must match one of the configured series labels.

data_description.widget_template: Widget layout. Use `value` for a single number, `progress` for a 0–1 bar, `gauge` for a value-in-range, `status` for a severity badge, or `stat_list` for up to 6 rows.
data_description.slug: Unique identifier for this widget. Leave empty to auto-generate from entity ID.

data_description.widget_name: Display name shown in the iOS widget picker. Defaults to the entity's friendly name.
data_description.value_attribute: Entity attribute holding the value. Leave empty to use the entity's primary state.
data_description.unit: Display unit shown next to the value. Max 32 characters.
data_description.value_scale: How to read the progress value. Auto treats a % unit or anything above 1 as a percent, and everything else as a 0.0-1.0 fraction. Pick percent or fraction to override. Progress template only.
data_description.min_value: Minimum value for the gauge range.
data_description.max_value: Maximum value for the gauge range.
data_description.stat_rows: Up to 6 rows. Format: `Label=entity_id[:attribute[:unit]]`, comma-separated. Example: `Users=sensor.user_count, Active=sensor.active_devices:count`. By default medium and large Home Screen widgets show all 6 rows; the small widget shows 4 and the Lock Screen rectangular shows 3 (both show up to 6 when every value is very short, like a single status glyph). Override this per widget in the PushWard app under Row Density (Auto, Compact, Comfortable).

data_description.label: Static label shown on the widget. Leave empty to derive from the entity.
data_description.label_attribute: Entity attribute used as the label. Overrides the static label.
data_description.subtitle_attribute: Entity attribute shown as the widget subtitle.
data_description.icon: Static icon. SF Symbols and MDI names supported.
data_description.icon_attribute: Entity attribute for a dynamic icon. Overrides the static icon.

data_description.accent_color: Accent color for the widget. Leave empty for the default.
data_description.accent_color_attribute: Entity attribute for a dynamic accent color.

data_description.tap_action_url: Optional URL opened when tapping the widget. Supports http(s) (opens Safari) or custom schemes like homeassistant://navigate/lovelace/0.
data_description.tap_action_foreground: On: tap opens the URL. Off: silent webhook (http(s) only) — fires a POST in the background.

data_description.widget_trigger_mode: How updates are triggered. `event` reacts to state changes; `poll` re-evaluates on a fixed interval.
data_description.widget_poll_interval: Interval between polls (10–3600 seconds). Only used when trigger mode is `poll`.

error.invalid_url: URL must include a valid scheme. Use http(s):// or a custom app scheme like homeassistant://.
error.silent_requires_http: Silent webhook (foreground off) requires an http:// or https:// URL.
error.invalid_gauge_range: Minimum value must be less than maximum value.
error.stat_rows_required: At least one stat row is required for the stat_list template.
error.invalid_stat_row: Each row must be label=entity_id (e.g. Users=sensor.user_count).
error.too_many_stat_rows: A stat list supports at most 6 rows.

abort.already_configured: A widget with this slug already exists.

deprecated_update_activity.title: The PushWard update_activity action is deprecated
deprecated_update_activity.description: The `pushward.update_activity` action is deprecated. Switch your automations to the template-specific action instead — for example `pushward.update_activity_countdown` or `pushward.update_activity_gauge`. The old action still works for now but will be removed in a future release.

usage_limit_notifications.title: PushWard notification limit reached
usage_limit_notifications.description: Your PushWard account has used {used} of its {limit} notifications for the current period, so new notifications will be rejected until the quota resets ({resets_at}). Upgrade your subscription in the PushWard iOS app to raise the limit.

usage_limit_live_activity.title: PushWard Live Activity update limit reached
usage_limit_live_activity.description: Your PushWard account has used {used} of its {limit} Live Activity updates for the current period, so new updates will be rejected until the quota resets ({resets_at}). Upgrade your subscription in the PushWard iOS app to raise the limit.

usage_limit_widgets.title: PushWard widget update limit reached
usage_limit_widgets.description: Your PushWard account has used {used} of its {limit} widget updates for the current period, so new updates will be rejected until the quota resets ({resets_at}). Upgrade your subscription in the PushWard iOS app to raise the limit.

usage_limit_emails.title: PushWard email limit reached
usage_limit_emails.description: Your PushWard account has used {used} of its {limit} emails for the current period, so new emails will be rejected until the quota resets ({resets_at}). Upgrade your subscription in the PushWard iOS app to raise the limit.
